data.body:
G3 container coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × this child 8937 "Producer<->consumer snapshot contract: shared schema + round-trip test through production path"), cross-checked against the full sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57).

This child is the KEYSTONE and FULLY covers:
- Parent AC1 ("A shared, single-source-of-truth snapshot-entry schema exists; both producer output and consumer reads validate against it") → 8937 AC1 (`_snapshot_schema.py` TypedDict + JSON Schema SSOT) + AC2 (fetch_snapshot validated against schema; would FAIL on pre-fix conditions).
- Parent AC2 ("One round-trip contract test exercises fetcher → snapshot → differs → applier through the production path, using fixtures derived from real Jira payloads") → 8937 AC3 (round-trip fetcher→differs→applier through production path; fails if producer key/shape diverges from a consumer read). Fixtures themselves are correctly delegated to fe57 (depends_on declared).
- Parent SC1 ("Producer and consumers can no longer drift on snapshot shape without a test failing") → directly satisfied by the joint round-trip contract; reinforced by fe57 (honest fixtures/fake) and 59ef (verified-fake honesty).
It also contributes to SC5/AC7 via 8937 AC4 (semantic assertions; no whole-blob golden equality / no mock-call-count).

All other parent criteria this child does not own are correctly delegated to named siblings: AC3/SC2 → 33d0 (collapse appliers); AC4/SC3 → 2359 (sub-op telemetry + silent-no-op canary); AC5/SC4 → 822a (hermetic bidirectional round-trip + de-encode expected-bug guards); fixtures/fake foundation → fe57; verified-fake honesty → 59ef. No per-criterion coverage gap is attributable to child 8937 itself; its declared OUT-of-scope items (fixtures→fe57, applier collapse→33d0, telemetry→2359) are consistent with the roster.

ONE ROSTER-LEVEL ABSENCE (re-cross-checked against the WHOLE roster including this child; already raised by Iso Tester on siblings 33d0, 59ef, and 822a — restated here for this pairing's record because 8937 is repeatedly named as its natural home):
- finding: Parent AC6 ("A documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNS new tests — no mock-call-count, no whole-blob golden equality; assert semantic round-trip + structure/type") is only PARTIALLY covered. Every child, including this one, only APPLIES the anti-change-detector posture to its OWN tests; no sibling owns a deliverable that PRODUCES a documented governing guardrail/policy artifact that governs FUTURE/new reconciler tests.
  - criteria: parent AC6
  - location: parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 Acceptance Criteria bullet 6; cross-checked against siblings 2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57
  - evidence: child 8937 AC4 "All assertions are semantic (keys/types/round-trip), not whole-blob golden equality or interaction/mock-call-count" is self-scoped to THIS story's own tests; 2359 AC4, 33d0 AC4, 822a AC4, 59ef AC3, and fe57 are likewise self-scoped; no sibling defines/produces a documented guardrail that governs new/future tests. 8937 is repeatedly cited (by siblings 33d0/59ef/822a) as AC6's natural home, yet 8937's own ACs do not include it.
  - scenarios: a new reconciler test added next cycle has no documented guardrail to conform to; the anti-change-detector intent silently erodes once these stories close
  - impact: parent AC6 cannot be fully checked off by closing the current children as written (4-bucket: PARTIALLY covered — posture applied per-test, documented governing artifact absent)
  - checklist_item: union of children covers each parent acceptance/success criterion
  - suggested_fix: add the documented-guardrail deliverable to one story (natural home: this child 8937, the contract keystone — e.g. a new AC: "a documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests") or add a small dedicated child, so AC6's documented governing artifact has an owner.

4-bucket summary for this pairing: AC1/AC2/SC1 FULLY covered by this child; SC5/AC7 contributed; AC3/AC4/AC5/SC2/SC3/SC4 covered by named siblings; AC7 structural/aggregate; AC6 PARTIALLY covered (single roster-level absence above). No coverage finding attributable to child 8937 itself for its owned criteria.
